steps.title: Identify what supporting the claim requires
steps.content: The LNH predicts a negative correlation between exotericity and grammatical complexity (measured by fusion and informativity). To say the outcome is inconsistent with the LNH, the data must show the opposite — a positive (or non-negative) correlation.

steps.title: Match the finding
steps.content:
Choice C describes a slightly positive correlation between fusion and exotericity and between informativity and exotericity, which directly contradicts the LNH's predicted inverse relationship.

The other choices either don't compare exotericity with the complexity indices or describe correlations consistent with the LNH.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
